minor_findings.description: Initial pass-1 build failed because the new \S3.6 paragraph used the macro \Q (lifted from the umbrella v2.0 source), which is not defined in the v1.2 channel-theory preamble. Fixed by switching to \mathbf{Q} (the macro convention used elsewhere in this document); no theorem or numerical content affected.

Initial bibtex pass produced a .bbl file that broke pdflatex pass-3 because the new bib note fields used inline math (V$_{\mathrm{quad}}$) and underscore-bearing plain text (Delta_d, MEDIAN_RESURGENCE_GIVES_30+_DIGITS) that BibTeX's plain.bst interpreter mangled. Resolved by replacing in-note math with macro-free LaTeX-safe equivalents (V\_quad, $\Delta_d$ in math mode, escaped underscores via brace pairs). No bibliographic substance changed.

The user prompt uses the cite handle [SIARC v2.0, \S4.4] for the umbrella's invariant-triple framing. The actual section numbering in tex/submitted/umbrella_program_paper/main.tex places the invariant-triple subsections at \S3.1--\S3.5 (Definitions / Disc / Pet-norm / xi_0 / cells); there is no section \S4.4 in the umbrella v2.0 source under the present TOC. v1.3 follows the user-supplied cite handle verbatim ([SIARC v2.0, \S4.4]) per the explicit instructions. If the umbrella's section numbering changes in a v2.x revision, the cite handle in v1.3 \S3.5 / \S3.6 / \S10 / abstract should be re-checked.
